prose:
Computed Fukui reactivity indices for benzaldehyde (C₇H₆O) with chemkit skill `fukui-reactivity`.

Method provenance
Property: Condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor; most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites.
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted (PySCF backend).
Geometry provenance: single-point on user-supplied geometry `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz` (no re-optimization; DFT//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (PySCF 2.x under the hood; density-fitting on by default).
Convergence & caveats: SCFs for N, N±1 states reported converged. See warnings below (charge-conservation checks). No explicit optimizer used (single-point). Live engine log written during run to the default `<cwd>/fukui_*.out` file (path echoed at launch).

Key numerical results (atomic indices are 0-based in JSON, 1-based labels in parentheses)
• Most electrophilic site: C (index 2, atom 3) with f⁺ = 0.39938963.
• Most nucleophilic site: O (index 0, atom 1) with f⁻ = 0.23839906.
• Σf⁺ = 1.0000; Σf⁻ = 1.0000 (charge-conservation checks passed).

Warnings (verbatim from tool result)
1. sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
2. sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
3. common header present

The headline value reported above is the largest electrophilic Fukui index (f⁺) of 0.39938963 for atom 3 (C).
